key_insight: Stable inorganic materials occupy a highly recurrent, compressible set of structural frameworks, with many chemically distinct compounds reusing the same prototypes.
engineering_relevance: A recurrence-based prototype catalog can help materials engineers prioritize likely crystal frameworks when searching for new stable compounds.
method: Analyze 23,160 thermodynamically stable inorganic structures on the convex hull.
Group structures into distinct frameworks and identify recurring ones as structural prototypes.
Compare recurrence patterns across metallic and ionic subsets.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 6,820 distinct frameworks among 23,160 stable structures, with 2,382 recurring prototypes; in filtered subsets, 6,270 metallic systems map to 535 frameworks and 327 prototypes, while 5,557 ionic compounds map to 1,704 frameworks and 696 prototypes.
watch_item: Framework recurrence is an empirical measure and may depend on dataset filtering, structure representation choices, and how chemically distinct materials are defined.
paper_title: The Recurrent Structural Frameworks of Stable Inorganic Materials
